    "origin": "The park was created on the site of the old Brooklyn Navy Yard, which had been closed since 1881. The land was purchased by the city in 1864 and the park was officially opened to the public in 1868.",
    "design_inspiration": "Olmsted and Vaux designed the park as a 'rural' escape from the rapidly industrializing city, drawing inspiration from English landscape gardens. The design features a central lake, winding paths, and a mix of open meadows and dense woodlands.",
    "cultural_significance": "The park became a beloved public space for Brooklyn residents, serving as a venue for concerts, sports, and community gatherings. It was also featured in the 1999 film 'You Can't Take It With You' and has been a popular filming location for movies and TV shows.",

      "Prospect Park Lake",
      "The Bandshell",
